Guy's coworkers, who frequented the bar, had shared stories of hearing racial slurs she had thrown at Guy. They had also relayed these offensive exchanges to Guy's family. Winston recognized her from those recounts, the complaints about the woman named Isolde vivid in his memory.

Meeting her gaze, he gestured to the collection of martial arts DVDs displayed at the bar. "Into martial arts movies, I see."

Isolde’s eyes lit up momentarily, "Absolutely. Their craft, the plot twist…nothing beats it!" She was known to vouch for these movies, frequently recommending them to curious customers. Her deep love for these films was apparent as she quoted lines from them frequently.

Detective Winston was indoors on another stifling California afternoon. His attention was fixed on the Irving case, a case that kept him on his toes for several days now. A screenwriter suspected in the murder of a fellow gym goer. He couldn’t help but feel the increasing pressure of the time crunch.

Irving was not an ordinary man. Winston had discovered from the interviews and police records that the guy particularly had some weird habits. Irving, it seemed, was battling insomnia and was unable to sleep in his own home at night. During these restless nights, he would busy himself with his notebooks.
